title: Vincetoxicum helferi
intro: Vincetoxicum helferi, also known as Helfer's periploca, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae, native to the Himalayan region. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum helferi include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is often used to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum helferi is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions. The plant has also been classified as a member of the genus Cynanchum by some sources, but it is widely recognized under the genus Vincetoxicum. The classification under Vincetoxicum is based on its distinct characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. This classification is widely accepted in botanical literature and research.

The plant is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating various health issues. It is also used to treat fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
